explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while vitamins are the biggest drag. It lands in S tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in S tier with an overall score of 76 from a base nutrition score of 76. Context barely changed the result.

topPros.title: can help meals feel more complete and usable
topPros.explanation: Fats sometimes improve the whole plate, not just their own stats.

topPros.title: small amounts can change a whole meal
topPros.explanation: That leverage matters when the fat quality is good.

topCons.title: extremely calorie dense by default
topCons.explanation: That is the unavoidable category burden.

topCons.title: not a complete food on its own
topCons.explanation: Even the better fats are still just part of a meal.

closing.summary: Canola Oil has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
closing.useCaseNote: Best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

reviewChecklist: Verify nutrient values are sourced well enough for publishable use.
Review narration for category repetition and trim any awkward lines.
Confirm subtitle density stays readable at phone speed.
Replace placeholder hero/sprite art with final asset selection.
Check that the final tier verdict feels editorially defensible.
nextHumanStep: Review the generated narration + manifest, then use the manifest as the handoff into visual assembly / editing.
